memory.summary: That scholarship money would really help, but doing it alone feels heavy compared to hanging out with my friends. I'm tempted to just grab some fun now instead of dealing with this solo work.
Starting this now buys me breathing room for those annoying uploads and essay tweaks later, plus I can totally reschedule the fun without losing anything.

I pushed through the solo work despite the fatigue, but seeing how little progress I actually made makes me realize I'm still avoiding the real task. It feels like I'm just burning energy on a half-finished start while my friends are probably having fun.

I'm stuck in a loop where I dread the solo scholarship work enough to miss immediate fun, yet my low energy makes actual progress feel impossible. It feels like I'm just spinning my wheels while everyone else enjoys life.

That tiny 0.02 progress feels like a victory now, proving I can actually move forward without the dread paralyzing me. Maybe this solo grind isn't so bad if I just stop overthinking it.

I pushed through the exhaustion to move the needle from 79% to completion, proving that my dread is just noise I can ignore. For once, the solo grind felt like a win instead of a betrayal of my friends.
I'm exhausted and barely moved the needle on my scholarship, but I'll just push through this last bit tomorrow so I can finally get back to hanging out with my friends.
That tiny 0.02 progress feels like a victory now, proving I can actually move forward without the dread paralyzing me. Maybe this solo grind isn't so bad if I just stop overthinking it.
I'm drained but the tiny progress on my scholarship feels like a win; I'll keep this momentum so I can finally drop everything and hang out with my friends later.

desires: To immediately drop the application and join my friends so I don't feel like the 'reliable' one who steals all the fun.
To prove to myself that I can finish this last bit of work now, rather than dragging it out until tomorrow when I'll be too tired to care about anything.
To avoid looking unreliable to my team by getting this done quickly, even if it means sacrificing sleep again.

goals.description: Finish the last 0.15 of the essay and upload files RIGHT NOW, then text Cohort 3 immediately to join me for food so I don't have to sit here feeling like the 'reliable' one who steals all the fun.

Even though my brain is fried and procrastination bias is high (I'm only at 67%), the emotional cost of missing out on my friends tonight feels infinitely worse than risking the deadline. I need to crush this 'admin block' solo right now just to reclaim my social energy without looking like I'm slacking off.

agent.last_reasoning: I need to crush this final admin block solo now so I can text Cohort 3 immediately without feeling like the 'reliable' one who steals fun. This advances g1, but I'm ignoring my sleep debt since relationships matter more tonight.
